author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks to the “Corn of wheat,” because He willed to die and to be multiplied: thanks to the only Son of God, our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, who disdained not to undergo our death, in order that He might make us worthy of His life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold Him that was single until He went hence; as He said in another Psalm, “Single I am until I go hence;” for He was a single corn of wheat in such sort as that He had in Himself a great fruitfulness of increase; in how many corns imitating the Passion of Him we exult, when we celebrate the nativities of the Martyrs!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many therefore members of Him, under one Head our Saviour Himself, being bound together in the bond of love and peace (as ye judge it fit that ye know, for ye have often heard), are one man: and of the same, as of one man, the voice is ofttimes heard, in the Psalms, and thus one crieth as though it were all, because all in one are one.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is then in this Psalm the voice of men troubled, and so indeed of Martyrs amid sufferings in peril, but relying on their own Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear them, and speak with them out of sympathy of heart, though it be not with similarity of suffering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are already crowned, we are still in peril: not that such sort of persecutions do vex us as have vexed them, but worse perchance in the midsts of all kinds of so great scandals.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our own times do more abound in that woe, which the Lord cried: “Woe to the world because of scandals.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, “Because iniquity hath abounded, the love of man shall wax cold.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not even that holy Lot at Sodom suffered corporal persecution from any one, or had it been told him that he should not dwell there: the persecution of him were the evil doings of the Sodomites.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then that Christ sitteth in Heaven, now that He is glorified, now that necks of kings are made subject to His yoke, and their brows placed beneath His sign, now that not any one remaineth to dare openly to trample upon Christians, still, however, we groan amid instruments and singers, still those enemies of the Martyrs, because with words and steel they have no power, with their own wantonness do persecute them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And O that we were sorrowing for Heathens alone: it would be some sort of comfort, to wait for those that not yet have been signed with the Cross of Christ; when they should be signed, and when, by His authority attached, they should cease to be mad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We see besides men wearing on their brow the sign of Him, at the same time on that same brow wearing the shamelessness of wantonness, and on the days and celebrations of the Martyrs not exulting but insulting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And amid these things we groan, and this is our persecution, if there is in us the love which saith, “Who is weak, and I am not weak?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is scandalized, and I burn not?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not any servant of God, then, is without persecution: and that is a true saying which the Apostle saith, “But even all men that will to live godly in Christ, shall suffer persecution.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, to my aid make speed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For need we have for an everlasting aid in this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when have we not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now however being in tribulation, let us especially say, “O God, to my aid make speed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be confounded and fear that seek my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ is speaking: whether Head speak or whether Body speak; He is speaking that hath said, “Why persecutest thou Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is speaking that hath said, “Inasmuch as ye have done it to one of the least of Mine, to Me ye have done it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The voice then of this Man is known to be of the whole man, of Head and of Body: that need not often be mentioned, because it is known.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be they confounded,” he saith, “and fear that seek my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In another Psalm He saith, “I was looking unto the right and saw, and there was not one that would know Me: flight hath perished from Me, and there is not one to seek out My soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There of persecutors He saith, that there was not one to seek out His soul: but here, “Let them be confounded and fear that seek My soul.”…And where is that which thou hast heard from thy Lord, “Love ye your enemies, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them that persecute you”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold thou sufferest persecution, and cursest them from whom thou sufferest: how dost thou imitate the Passions of thy Lord that have gone before, hanging on the cross and saying, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To persons saying such things the Martyr replieth and saith, thou hast set before me the Lord, saying, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do:” understand thou my voice also, in order that it may be thine too: for what have I said concerning mine enemies?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be confounded and fear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already such vengeance hath been taken on the enemies of the Martyrs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Saul that persecuted Stephen, he was confounded and feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was breathing out slaughters, he was seeking some to drag and slay: a voice having been heard from above, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou Me,” he was confounded and laid low, and he was raised up to obedience, that had been inflamed unto persecuting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then the Martyrs desire for their enemies, “Let them be confounded and fear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so long as they are not confounded and fear, they must needs defend their actions: glorious they think themselves, because they hold, because they bind, because they scourge, because they kill, because they dance, because they insult, and because of all these doings they be some time confounded and fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they be confounded, they will also be converted: because converted they cannot be, unless they shall have been confounded and shall have feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then wish these things to our enemies, let us wish them without fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold I have said, and let me have said it with you, may all that still dance and sing and insult the Martyrs “be confounded and fear:” at last within these walls confounded may they beat their breasts!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be turned away backward and blush that think evil things to me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At first there was the assault of them persecuting, now there hath remained the malice of them thinking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fact, there are in the Church distinct seasons of persecutions following one another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was made an assault on the Church when kings were persecuting: and because kings had been foretold as to persecute and as to believe, when one had been fulfilled the other was to follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There came to pass also that which was consequent; kings believed, peace was given to the Church, the Church began to be set in the highest place of dignity, even on this earth, even in this life: but there is not wanting the roar of persecutors, they have turned their assaults into thoughts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In these thoughts, as in a bottomless pit, the devil hath been bound, he roareth and breaketh not forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it hath been said concerning these times of the Church, “The sinner shall see, and shall be angry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And shall do what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which he did at first?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Drag, bind, smite?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doeth not this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With his teeth he shall gnash, and shall pine away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And with these men the Martyr is, as it were, angry, and yet for these men the Martyr prayeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in like manner as he hath wished well to those men concerning whom he hath said, “Let them be confounded and fear that seek my soul:” so also now, “Let them be turned backward, and blush, that think evil things to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In order that they may not go before, but follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that censureth the Christian religion, and on his own system willeth to live, willeth as it were to go before Christ, as though He indeed had erred and had been weak and infirm, because He either willed to suffer or could suffer in the hands of the Jews; but that he is a clever man for guarding against all these things; in shunning death, even in basely lying to escape death, and slaying his soul that he may live in body, he thinketh himself a man of singular and prudent measures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goeth before in censuring Christ, in a manner he outstrippeth Christ: let him believe in Christ, and follow Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which had been desired but now for persecutors thinking evil things, the same the Lord Himself said to Peter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now in a certain place Peter willed to go before the Lord.…A little before, “Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona, for flesh and blood hath not revealed it to thee, but My Father which is in Heaven:” now in a moment, “Go back behind Me, Satan.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Go back behind Me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou willest to go before Me, thou willest to give Me counsel, it is better that thou follow My counsel: this is, “go back,” go back behind Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is silencing one outstripping, in order that he may go backward; and He is calling him Satan, because he willeth to go before the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A little before, “blessed;” now, “Satan.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence a little before, “blessed”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, “to thee,” He saith, “flesh and blood hath not revealed it, but My Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence now, “Satan”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “thou savourest not,” He saith, “the things which are of God, but the things which are of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then that would duly celebrate the nativities of the Martyrs, long for the imitation of the Martyrs; let us not wish to go before the Martyrs, and think ourselves to be of better understanding than they, because we shun sufferings in behalf of righteousness and faith which they shunned not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore be they that think evil things, and in wantonness feed their hearts, “turned backward and blush.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them hear from the Apostle afterwards saying, “But what fruit had ye some time in those things at which ye now blush?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be turned away forthwith blushing, that say to me, Well, well” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Two are the kinds of persecutors, revilers and flatterers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The tongue of the flatterer doth more persecute than the hand of the slayer: for this also the Scripture hath called a furnace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly when the Scripture was speaking of persecution, it said, “Like gold in a furnace it hath proved them” (speaking of Martyrs being slain), “and as the holocaust’s victim it hath received them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear how even the tongue of flatterers is of such sort: “The proving,” he saith, “of silver and of gold is fire; but a man is proved by the tongue of men praising him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is fire, this also is fire: out of both thou oughtest to go forth safe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The censurer hath broken thee, thou hast been broken in the furnace like an earthen vessel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Word hath moulded thee, and there hath come the trial of tribulation: that which hath been formed, must needs be seasoned; if it hath been well moulded, there hath come the fire to strengthen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence He said in the Passion, “Dried up like a potsherd hath been My virtue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Passion and the furnace of tribulation had made Him stronger.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what cometh to pass when they are all turned back and blush, whether it be they that seek my soul, or they that think evil things to me, or they that with perverse and feigned benevolence with tongue would soften the stroke which they inflict, when they shall have been themselves turned away and confounded; there shall come to pass what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them exult and be joyous in Thee:” not in me, not in this man or in that man; but in whom they have been made light that were darkness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them exult and be joyous in Thee, all that seek Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One thing it is to seek God, another thing to seek man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be joyous that seek Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They shall not be joyous then that seek themselves, whom Thou hast first sought before they sought Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet did that sheep seek the Shepherd, it had strayed from the flock, and He went down to it; He sought it, and carried it back upon His shoulders.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will He despise thee, O sheep, seeking Him, who hath first sought thee despising Him and not seeking Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then begin thou to seek Him that first hath sought thee, and hath carried thee back on His shoulders.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou that which He speaketh of, “They that are My sheep hear My voice, and follow Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then thou seekest Him that first hath sought thee, and hast become a sheep of His, and thou hearest the voice of thy Shepherd, and followest Him; see what He showeth to thee of Himself, what of His Body, in order that as to Himself thou mayest not err, as to the Church thou mayest not err, that no one may say to thee, that is Christ which is not Christ, or that is the Church which is not the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many men have said that Christ had no flesh, and that Christ hath not risen in His Body: do not thou follow the voices of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear thou the voice of Himself the Shepherd, that was clothed with flesh, in order that He might seek lost flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath risen again, and He saith, “Handle ye and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones as ye see Me have.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He showeth Himself to thee, the voice of Him follow thou.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He showeth also the Church, that no one may deceive thee by the name of Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It behoved,” He saith, “Christ to suffer, and to rise again from the dead the third day, and that there should be preached repentance and remission of sins through all nations, beginning with Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast the voice of Thy Shepherd, do not thou follow the voice of strangers: and a thief thou shalt not fear, if thou shalt have followed the voice of the Shepherd.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how shalt thou follow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou shalt neither have said to any man, as if it were by his own merit, Well, well: nor shalt have heard the same with joy, so that thy head be not made fat with the oil of a sinner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all them exult and be joyous in Thee, that seek Thee; and let them say”—let them say what, that exult?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be the Lord alway magnified!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let all them say this, that exult and seek Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be the Lord alway magnified; yea, they that love Thy salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only, “Be the Lord magnified;” but also, “alway.”…A sinner thou art, be He magnified in order that He may call; thou confessest, be He magnified in order that He may forgive: now thou livest justly, be He magnified in order that He may direct: thou perseverest even unto the end, be He magnified in order that He may glorify.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be the Lord,” then, “alway magnified; yea, they love His saving health.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from Him they have salvation, not from themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The saving health of the Lord our God, is the Saviour our Lord Jesus Christ: whosoever loveth the Saviour, confesseth himself to have been made whole; whosoever confesseth himself to have been made whole, confesseth himself to have been sick.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not their own saving health, as if they could save themselves of themselves: not as it were the saving health of a man, as though by him they could be saved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Do not,” he saith, “confide in princes, and in the sons of men, in whom there is no safety.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Of the Lord is safety, and upon Thy people is Thy blessing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, “Be the Lord magnified:” wilt thou never, wilt thou nowhere?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Him was something, in me nothing: but if in Him is whatsoever I am, be He, not I.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou then what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I am needy and poor” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is rich, He abounding, He needing nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold my light, behold whence I am illumined; for I cry, “Thou shalt illumine my candle, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then of thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I am needy and poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am like an orphan, my soul is like a widow destitute and desolate: help I seek, alway mine infirmity I confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There have been forgiven me my sins, now I have begun to follow the commandments of God: still, however, I am needy and poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why still needy and poor?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “I see another law in my members fighting against the law of my mind.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why needy and poor?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, “blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Still I hunger, still I thirst: my fulness hath been put off, not taken away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, aid Thou me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Most suitably also Lazarus is said to be interpreted, “one aided:” that needy and poor man, that was transported into the bosom of Abraham; and beareth the type of the Church, which ought alway to confess that she hath need of aid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is true, this is godly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have said to the Lord, My God Thou art.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my goods Thou needest not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He needeth not us, we need Him: therefore He is truly Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou art not the very true Lord of thy servant: both are men, both needing God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou supposest thy servant to need thee, in order that thou mayest give him bread; thou also needest thy servant, in order that he may aid thy labours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Each one of you doth need the other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore neither of you is truly lord, and neither of you truly servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear thou the true Lord, of whom thou art the true servant: “I have said to the Lord, My God Thou art.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why art Thou Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because my goods Thou needest not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what of thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I am needy and poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the needy and poor: may God feed, may God alleviate, may God aid: “O God,” he saith, “aid Thou me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My helper and deliverer art Thou; O Lord, delay not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art the helper and deliverer: I need succour, help Thou; entangled I am, deliver Thou.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no one will deliver from entanglings except Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There stand round about us the nooses of divers cares, on this side and on that we are torn as it were with thorns and brambles, we walk a narrow way, perchance we have stuck fast in the brambles: let us say to God, “Thou art my deliverer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that showed us the narrow way, hath taught us to follow it.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “delay not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because many men say, it is a long time till Christ comes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then: because we say, “delay not,” will He come before He hath determined to come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth this prayer, “delay not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May not Thy coming seem to me to be too long delayed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to thee it seemeth a long time, to God it seemeth not long, to whom a thousand years are one day, or the three hours of a watch.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou shalt not have had endurance, late for thee it will be: and when to thee it shall be late, thou wilt be diverted from Him, and wilt be like unto those that were wearied in the desert, and hastened to ask of God the pleasant things which He was reserving for them in the Land; and when there were not given on their journey the pleasant things, whereby perchance they would have been corrupted, they murmured against God, and went back in heart unto Egypt: to that place whence in body they had been severed, in heart they went back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not thou, then, so, do not so: fear the word of the Lord, saying, “Remember Lot’s wife.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She too being on the way, but now delivered from the Sodomites, looked back; in the place where she looked back, there she remained: she became a statue of salt, in order to season thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to thee she hath been given for an example, in order that thou mayest have sense, mayest not stop infatuated on the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe her stopping and pass on: observe her looking back, and do thou be reaching forth unto the things before, as Paul was.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it, not to look back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Of the things behind forgetful,” he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore thou followest, being called to the heavenly reward, whereof hereafter thou wilt glory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the same Apostle saith, “There remaineth for me a crown of righteousness, which in that day the Lord, the just Judge, shall render to me.”
